name: Roo Code (Roocode)
summary: Open-source AI developer platform combining a VS Code extension with autonomous cloud agents. Key differentiators: role-based Modes, orchestrator/boomergang recursive task decomposition, provider-agnostic router, MCP integrations, and action proposal/approval UX.

features.title: Mode-based Roles
features.description: Predefined Modes (Code, Architect, Ask, Debug, Test, Custom) that constrain model behavior and tool access.

features.title: Orchestrator / Recursive Task Decomposition
features.description: Planner/orchestrator breaks large tasks into subtasks and coordinates agents for multi-step workflows.

features.title: Cloud Agents
features.description: Persistent autonomous agents that can run tasks, create PRs, and interact with team systems.

features.title: VS Code Extension & Webview UI
features.description: Full-featured extension with webviews, diffs, and approval UI.

features.title: Provider Router & Adapters
features.description: Model-agnostic dispatch layer to multiple provider backends with cost tracking.

features.title: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Integration
features.description: Connects external tools/services as MCP servers with dynamic tool discovery.

features.title: Action Proposal + Approval Flow
features.description: Agents propose edits or commands which the user approves; Auto-approve for trusted workflows.

features.title: GitHub PR & Automation
features.description: Create PRs, run reviewers/fixers, comment on PRs, manage branches.

features.title: Token/Cost Tracking
features.description: Per-request token counts and cost estimates for budgeting and provider selection.

features.title: User-defined Skills & Slash Commands
features.description: Reusable workflows and slash command exposure (local ~/.roo/skills).

architecture.stack: TypeScript monorepo (pnpm, turbo); extension + cloud + router backends; webviews for UI

strengths_vs_ragent: Mature Mode system and UX for role switching
Robust multi-agent orchestration and autonomous PR workflows
Strong VS Code UX and cloud product
weaknesses_vs_ragent: TS/Node-first and heavy VS Code focus (ragent is Rust/CLI-first)
Possible coupling of cloud features to managed router (self-hosting complexity)
Less emphasis on Rust-native tooling and sourcemap handling

adoptable_features.notes: System prompts per mode, CLI flag or config to switch, custom modes storage.
Render diffs in TUI, require explicit approval; Auto-approve option.
Planner that emits subtasks; task queue and worker implementation.
Adapter pattern for providers and middleware to estimate token usage.
Start with HTTP/stdio transports for tool adapters.
Offer self-hosted runner first; optional managed cloud later.

poc_suggestions.title: Mode system POC
poc_suggestions.description: Add role-specific prompts and a CLI flag to switch modes.

poc_suggestions.title: Action proposal UI POC
poc_suggestions.description: Render diffs in TUI and require approval before applying.

poc_suggestions.title: MCP adapter POC
poc_suggestions.description: Small HTTP endpoint exposing run-shell and read-file to agents.

integration_notes.providers: Adapter interface for OpenAI/Anthropic/others; router middleware for cost estimates
integration_notes.mcp: Expose simple HTTP/JSON protocol for external tool integration; support stdio transport for local tools

missing_info_and_next_steps: Direct inspection of Roo Code monorepo (packages/extension, packages/cloud, packages/router, packages/mcp, packages/indexer)
Release notes and CHANGELOG for feature timelines
Provider router implementation details (which provider SDKs and how cost accounting works)
